title: Classic tiny LEGO computer brick design from 1979 made into full-scale working computer Mac Mini implanted into this 10:1 scale inflated reimagining
summary: A 10:1 scale working model of the classic LEGO computer brick has been created, housing a Mac Mini M4 inside. This inflated reimagining pays homage to the original design from 1979. The resulting computer is a functional and nostalgic tribute.
insight: Reviving retro design concepts with modern technology creates innovative possibilities.

title: OpenAI's massive Stargate data center canceled as firm can't reach terms with Oracle, operator struggles with reliability issues Meta said to be interested in snatching excess capacity
summary: OpenAI's massive Stargate data center expansion canceled due to financing issues. Oracle couldn't reach terms, while OpenAI struggles with reliability. Meta may take excess capacity.
insight: Reliability and financing woes hinder AI data center expansion efforts.

title: Car powered by 500 disposable vape batteries boasts 18-mile range, 35mph top speed, USB-C charging port early 2000s Reva G.Wiz gets a makeover
summary: A YouTuber built a car using 500 vape batteries, achieving 18 miles and 35mph. The vehicle features a USB-C charging port. A 2000s Reva G.Wiz electric car has been given a modern makeover.
insight: Recycling vape waste to power electric vehicles is a viable concept.

title: NASA successfully deflected a small asteroid with its DART rocket, kinetic strike ejected a significant amount of debris from the asteroid impact nudges the orbit of the small astronomical body, affecting the larger cosmic object as well
summary: NASA's DART mission successfully deflected a small asteroid by altering its orbit, impacting both the target and nearby cosmic bodies.
insight: Demonstrating asteroid deflection capabilities for near-Earth object protection and planetary defense.

title: Palmer Luckey s retro gaming venture seeks $1 billion valuation as FPGA-based Nintendo 64 clone launch nears ModRetro's M64 console plays original N64 cartridges, supports 4K resolution
summary: Palmer Luckey's ModRetro is seeking a $1 billion valuation, preparing to launch its M64 console, a Nintendo 64 clone that plays original cartridges and supports 4K resolution.
insight: Reviving retro gaming with cutting-edge tech drives innovation

title: Chinese SSD maker YMTC lists first commercial PCIe 5.0 SSD as worldwide shortage intensifies Xtacking 4.0 NAND powers speeds of up to 10,500 MB/s
summary: YMTC lists the PC550 SSD with PCIe 5.0 x4 connectivity for AI and commercial systems, offering speeds of up to 10,500 MB/s. This SSD features NVMe 2.0 support and a four-channel design. The new SSD is part of YMTC's Xtacking 4.0 NAND technology.
insight: YMTC's PCIe 5.0 SSD boosts speed for AI and commercial applications

title: US startup plans to build data centers inside ocean-based wind turbines, servers water cooled via chilly North Sea each leg houses a data center, firm set to launch three-legged prototype in Norway s North Sea this year
summary: Offshore wind turbines could house large data centers, powered by wind and cooled by cold ocean water.
insight: Sustainable data centers leveraging renewable energy and natural cooling sources.

title: Norwegian gov't consumer watchdog calls out enshittification of video games, connected devices, and others claims hardware deliberately degraded after purchase
summary: The Norwegian government's consumer watchdog reports that companies intentionally degrade products post-purchase through software updates. This affects vehicles, washing machines, and other connected devices. The report claims this practice, called 'enshittification,' hurts consumers.
insight: Companies deliberately degrade products with software updates to boost profits.

title: Jensen Huang set to receive $4 million bonus from Nvidia, 0.002% of his $164 billion net worth a small incentive for one of the richest people on earth
summary: Nvidia's compensation committee set a $4 million bonus for CEO Jensen Huang
insight: CEO's $4m bonus a small fraction of his $164 billion net worth

title: Seagate FireCuda X1070 SSD spotted at retailers listed at $829.99 before any official announcement
summary: Seagate's unannounced FireCuda X1070 NVMe SSD briefly appeared on Amazon and Best Buy.
insight: Early listing hints at imminent official Seagate storage product announcement

title: California sues websites hosting 3D printed gun files online platforms allegedly violate multiple civil codes regarding unlawful distribution and manufacturing of firearms
summary: California sues websites hosting 3D printed gun files, alleging unlawful distribution and manufacturing.
insight: Easy online access to 3D printed guns poses significant public safety risks.

title: Memory bit flips cause up to 15% of Firefox crashes, asserts Mozilla engineer figure inferred from 470,000 auto-submitted crash reports
summary: A Mozilla engineer analyzed 470,000 crash reports and found that up to 15% of Firefox crashes are caused by memory bit flips. This suggests a significant issue with the browser's stability and reliability. The engineer's analysis aims to help identify and fix the problem.
insight: Memory bit flips pose a significant threat to Firefox's stability and user experience.

title: SayoDevice O3C 3-Key Hall-effect keypad boasts 8,000Hz polling rate for playing rhythm games
summary: The SayoDevice O3C is a Hall-effect keypad designed for rhythm and single-input games, offering rapid trigger support and customizable actuation points. It features a 0.96-inch IPS color display, USB 2.0 connectivity, and an 8,000 Hz polling rate for low input latency.
insight: Optimized for low-latency gaming with high polling rate performance

title: STMicro STM32C5 entry-level, 144 MHz Cortex-M33 MCU features up to 1MB flash, 256KB SRAM, Ethernet, CAN Bus
summary: The STMicro STM32C5 MCU family is designed for industrial and IoT applications, featuring a 144 MHz Cortex-M33 core, up to 1MB flash, and 256KB SRAM, with enhanced security features and peripheral support.
insight: Enhanced security and performance in low-power, high-reliability industrial MCUs.

title: DSG-22.6 GHz is a $1,590 open-source RF signal generator based on Atek Midas s custom ICs (Crowdfunding)
summary: Atek Midas launched DSG-22.6 GHz, a high-performance open-source RF signal generator with a frequency range of 0.15 GHz to 22.6 GHz, offering professional-grade frequency synthesis at a fraction of the cost of traditional benchtop equipment.
insight: Low-cost, high-performance RF signal generator for makers and production environments

title: Here are the 15 products Apple just discontinued as new iPhones, iPads, and Macs arrive
summary: Apple has discontinued 15 products, likely to promote new flagship models. This move aims to simplify their product lineup and encourage buyers to upgrade to the latest offerings. The discontinued products include iPhones, iPads, and Macs that are no longer supported or sold.
insight: Focusing on latest products drives sales and simplifies Apple's offerings

title: Experimental lithium-metal battery delivers 700 Wh/kg and works in extreme cold
summary: Researchers have developed an experimental lithium-metal battery with 700 Wh/kg capacity, working well in extreme cold temperatures.
insight: Improving electrolyte chemistry boosts battery efficiency and cold-weather performance.

title: Nintendo sues the US government over "unlawful" Trump-era tariffs
summary: Nintendo is suing the US government over 'unlawful' Trump-era tariffs, seeking refunds and interest.
insight: Nintendo seeks compensation for tariffs struck down by the Supreme Court ruling.

title: The MacBook Neo is a $500 Wake-Up Call for the Entire PC Industry
summary: Apple's MacBook Neo is a significant announcement for the PC industry, targeting budget markets. The $599 laptop is not for individual consumers but holds importance for the industry. It might spark a price war among budget Windows laptops.
insight: Budget Windows market faces a $599 price benchmark threat
